ai-providers-desc: rmhcode supports multiple AI coding backends. By default, it uses Claude.
auth-subheading-desc: Generate a token above, then use it with the CLI. Or use the browser-based flow:
auto-create-repo-desc-suffix: to create a GitHub repository, push your code, and publish — all in one step:
auto-generate-desc-suffix: in any project directory scans your codebase and generates a
binary-desc: Pre-built binaries — no Node.js required. Download, make executable (
binary-desc-2: on Linux/macOS), and place in your PATH.
browse-builds-desc: Explore projects created by the community with rmhcode
cli-auth-desc: Generate a token to link rmhcode with your RMH account, or use the browser login flow.
cli-requesting-access: CLI is requesting access to your account
cmd-edit-build-desc: Edit an existing build by its slug
cmd-list-builds-desc: Browse and manage your published builds (interactive menu)
cmd-login-desc: Authenticate with your RMH account (browser flow or --token)
cmd-logout-desc: Sign out and remove stored token
cmd-push-build-create-repo-desc: Create a GitHub repo, push code, and publish in one step
cmd-push-build-desc: Publish a project to User Builds (interactive prompts)
deepwiki-desc: AI-powered documentation for any public GitHub repository.
feature-github-repo-desc: Create a GitHub repo, push code, and publish a build in one step with --create-repo.
feature-mcp-desc: Bundled DeepWiki and GitHub MCP servers for docs and repo interaction.
feature-multi-provider-desc: Choose between Claude, OpenAI Codex, or Google Gemini as your coding backend.
feature-rmh-account-desc: Sign in with your rmhstudios.com account via browser or token flow.
feature-tmux-desc: Launch a 3-pane tmux session with rmhcode and a shell, auto-installing tmux if needed.
feature-user-builds-desc: Publish your creations to the community showcase with a single command.
github-mcp-desc: Interact with GitHub issues, pull requests, and repositories directly from the CLI.
hero-description: AI-powered coding assistant with RMH integrations. Build projects with Claude, Codex, or Gemini and publish to the User Builds showcase.
init-item-project-name: Project name (from package.json, Cargo.toml, pyproject.toml, etc.)
init-item-tech-stack: Tech stack detection (frameworks, databases, testing tools, styling)
install-note-uninstall: Note: may require an uninstall before reinstalling for updates.
invalid-auth-request-description: This page should be opened from the rmhcode CLI. Missing required parameters.
invalid-callback-url: Invalid callback URL. Only localhost is allowed.
manual-win-path-note-2: to your PATH and create a
mcp-desc: rmhcode comes with bundled MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers that are automatically configured during installation.
perm-user-builds: Create and manage your User Builds
rmh-builds-desc-prefix: Publish and manage projects on the
sign-in-description: You need to sign in to your RMH account to authorize the CLI.
sign-in-to-generate: Sign in to generate a CLI token
token-expiry-notice: Token expires in 30 days. You can revoke access anytime from your account settings.
uninstall-windows-installer: Windows installer: Use Add/Remove Programs or run the uninstaller from the Start Menu.
usage-banner-note-2: to always suppress the banner. All standard Claude Code arguments and flags work as normal.
